gui.add.warning.title: Config warnings
gui.add.warning.help: Informational only — you can still save the config.
gui.add.warning.no_dns: No DNS servers configured — system DNS will leak outside the tunnel.
gui.add.warning.conflicting_include: Peer advertises 0.0.0.0/0 but routing mode is Include — no traffic will reach the tunnel.
gui.add.warning.low_mtu: MTU is below 1280 — the tunnel may fail to carry standard packets.
gui.add.warning.high_mtu: MTU is above 1500 — larger than typical Ethernet will fragment.
gui.add.warning.no_keepalive_nat: No PersistentKeepalive set — NAT traversal may drop after idle periods.
gui.add.warning.multi_peer_unsupported: Multiple [Peer] sections found — only the first peer will be used.
gui.add.warning.ipv4_only_addresses: Interface has no IPv4 address — IPv4 traffic cannot use the tunnel.
gui.delete.title: Delete tunnel?
gui.delete.body: Delete {name}? Its .conf and rules file will be removed.
gui.prefs.hooks_description: Enable PreUp / PostUp / PreDown / PostDown hooks for .conf files. Hooks run as root in the helper — enable only if you trust every .conf and every user on this machine.
gui.prefs.launch_at_login_description: Start SplitWG automatically when you log in.
gui.prefs.kill_switch.label: Enable kill switch
gui.prefs.kill_switch.description: Block all non-tunnel traffic while a tunnel is active. Applies on the next connect.
gui.prefs.kill_switch.first_use_warning: If SplitWG crashes with a tunnel up, run `sudo pfctl -a splitwg -F all` in Terminal to clear the block.
gui.prefs.kill_switch.apply_hint: Takes effect on the next connect; currently active tunnels are unaffected.
gui.export.title: Export configs
gui.export.description: Select tunnels to bundle into an AES-256 encrypted package.
gui.export.min_length_warning: Password must be at least 12 characters.
gui.import.title: Import package
gui.prefs.updates.auto_check_description: Checks GitHub Releases every 24 hours for new versions. No data is sent.
notify.update.available_body: Version {version} is available. Open Preferences to install.
gui.update.modal.title: SplitWG update
gui.update.modal.quit_notice: SplitWG will quit and reopen automatically.
notify.launch_at_login.requires_approval: Launch at login registered — approve it in System Settings → General → Login Items.
notify.kill_switch.enabled: Kill switch enabled. Takes effect on the next connect.
notify.kill_switch.disabled: Kill switch disabled. Takes effect on the next connect.
gui.prefs.geodb.description: Downloads GeoLite2 MMDB files from GitHub once a day. Used for `country:XX` rules.
notify.geodb.up_to_date: GeoIP database is up to date.
notify.drop.multiple_ignored: Ignored {count} extra file(s); only the first was imported.
notify.drop.not_a_conf: Dropped file is not a WireGuard .conf — ignored.
notify.hook_scripts_state: Hook scripts {state}. Takes effect on the next connect.
notify.tunnel_hooks_state: Hooks {state} for {name}. Takes effect on the next connect.
notify.language_changed.title: SplitWG
prefs.confirm.enable.title: Enable hook scripts?
prefs.confirm.enable.body:
SplitWG will execute PreUp / PostUp / PreDown / PostDown commands from your .conf files AS ROOT.

Anyone with write access to a file in ~/.config/splitwg/ could execute arbitrary code as root.

Only enable this if you trust every .conf in that directory and the users on this machine.

Takes effect on the NEXT connect; currently active tunnels are unaffected.
prefs.confirm.disable.title: Disable hook scripts?
prefs.confirm.disable.body:
New connections will stop executing PreUp / PostUp / PreDown / PostDown from your .conf files.

Active tunnels keep running with the hooks they were started with; the change takes effect on the next connect.
